steps.title: Check current flagger deployment
steps.description:
Verify your flagger version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flagger -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flagger
helm list -n flagger 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working flagger installation.

steps.title: Review flagger configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant flagger configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n flagger -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flagger
kubectl get configmap -n flagger -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=flagger
```
[Keda](https://keda.sh/) is a useful tool for doing event-driven autoscaling in Kubernetes. To do its job, you create [ScaledObject manifests](https://keda.sh/docs/2.6/scalers/activemq/), and Keda then creates and manages the HPAs itself.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Keda Support
steps.description:
Flagger added KEDA ScaledObject support, allowing canary deployments to work with KEDA-managed autoscaling. This enables Flagger to pause and resume KEDA scaling during progressive delivery rollouts.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flagger -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flagger
kubectl get events -n flagger --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Keda Support" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Flagger added KEDA ScaledObject support, allowing canary deployments to work with KEDA-managed autoscaling. This enables Flagger to pause and resume KEDA scaling during progressive delivery rollouts.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with flagger installed or the issue environment reproducible.
